title: Brimonidine Ophthalmic: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:

This medication may be prescribed for other uses; ask your doctor or pharmacist for more information.
What special precautions should I follow? 
Before using brimonidine eye drops, tell your doctor or pharmacist if you are allergic to brimonidine eye drops or any other medications. Tell your doctor and pharmacist what prescription and nonprescription medications, vitamins, nutritional supplements, and herbal products you are taking or plan to take while using brimonidine eye drops. Your doctor may need to change the doses of your medications or monitor you carefully for side effects. if you are using any other topical eye medications, instill them 5 minutes before or 5 minutes after you instill brimonidine eye drops. tell your doctor if you often feel dizzy when you sit or stand from a lying position and if you have or have ever had depression; conditions that affect your blood circulation including Raynaud's disease (a condition that causes attacks of low blood circulation to the fingers and toes), thromboangiitis obliterans (a condition that causes poor blood circulation in the hands and feet), and problems with blood flow to your heart or brain; or heart, kidney, or liver disease. tell your doctor if yo
